summary: Oracle consultation register from Pachacamac — a record of who came to consult the oracle, what they asked, and what they paid. Cord 1 (INT=41) carries 96 subsidiaries = the detailed register of 41 sessions. The body (cords 4-96) lists ~53 individual consultations with text labels (subject) and numbers (offerings). Subjects: terrain, lineage, bridges, work, secrets. The secret (paka) commands the highest offering (279). Unique 'spiral knots' may be per-consultation validation seals. 226 cords, 9 colors, 95.7% coverage.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  226 cords: 106 INT (sum=5156), 116 STRING, 4 EMPTY.
  6 clusters, 4 depth levels (L1-L4), paired architecture.
  Ethnologisches Museum, Berlin. Provenance: Pachacamac.
  Urton notes: 'unusual spiral knots with and without axis.'
  Coverage: 95.7% (111/116 STRING cords confirmed).
  9 colors: W(83), AB(58), BG(21), MB(20), LC(19), KB(11), VR(5), YB(4), GG(2).

INTERPRETATION: ORACLE CONSULTATION REGISTER
  Pachacamac was the greatest oracle of the Andes — a pilgrimage site
  where people paid to consult the god. The priests kept records.
  This khipu is one of those records: who came, what they asked,
  how much they paid.

THE HEADER — CORD 1 (96 subsidiaries, 4 levels)
  L1: INT=41 [W] — 41 consultations registered.
  The subsidiary tree is the DETAILED REGISTER of these 41 sessions.
  Each block in the tree follows a pattern:
    - TEXT cord (L2) = the SUBJECT of the consultation
    - INT cords = the OFFERING paid
    - L3/L4 subsidiaries = details, sub-questions, breakdowns
  Colors encode roles:
    AB (subject) = the consultant (who comes)
    BG (identity) = their identifier / ayllu
    LC (ritual) = the question posed to the oracle
    MB (category) = the type of consultation
    W (white) = the price paid
    VR (vermilion) = special/urgent cases
    GG (justice) = judicial consultations

  KEY ENTRIES IN THE TREE:
    Block 3: wapa (administrator) = 28. An official consults. Tariff: 28.
    Block 4: pata (terrain) = 21+62+47+24+19+27 = 200. Land question.
      Six offerings in six colors — the most detailed entry.
    Block 6: chana (canal) = 17+102+48+22 = 189. Canal question.
    Block 7: papa (father) + L3 kaka (uncle) = 76+60 = 136.
      Lineage question: father and maternal uncle. Offering: 136.
    Block 10: wata (year) + L3 taka-1OBJ (to strike ME) = 54.
      Threat consultation: someone fears being struck. Offering: 54.
    Block 11: way (path) + L3 pata (terrain) = 92+23 = 115.
      Path to a terrain. Navigation/access question.
    Block 14: GEN-ti + L3 wayta (flower) + L3 paka-1OBJ (MY SECRET)
      = 20+45+82+26+67+39 = 279. HIGHEST OFFERING IN THE TREE.
      The one who comes with a secret pays the most.
    Block 22: waka (huaca) = 28+70 = 98. Question about the huaca itself.
    Block 24: ACC-waka + L3 pama (formula) = 55+72 = 127.
      The huaca receives the ritual formula. Offering: 127.

THE BODY — INDIVIDUAL CONSULTATIONS (cords 4-96)
  ~53 entries, each: text label (subject) + number(s) (offering).
  The oracle opens each session: 1OBJ-pipa — 'to me, who are you?'

  NOTABLE ENTRIES:
    #2: maki (labor) = 100. Consultation about work. Offering: 100.
    #5: tata (father) = 31. Lineage question. Offering: 31.
    #10: taka-ACC (assault) = 54. Violence case. Tariff: 54.
    #21: pama-PASS (formula received) = 76. The oracle has spoken.
    #29: 1OBJ-cha (me, small/dear) = 120+61+59 = 240.
      HIGHEST OFFERING IN THE BODY. Someone desperate, speaking of
      themselves with tenderness. The diminutive -cha marks distress.
    #35: wakama (toward the huaca) = 55+16+57 = 128.
      Meta-consultation: asking the oracle about the oracle.
    #39: chapa (guardian) = 56. Question about a guardian.
    #40: taki-ACC (ritual chant). Question about a ceremony.
    #41: PASS-chaka (bridge, passive). The bridge has been... destroyed?
    #51: chaka (bridge) = 84+9 = 93. The bridge again.
    #53: 1OBJ-ku (myself) = 38. The administrator's closing signature.

CONSULTATION SUBJECTS (ranked by frequency)
    papa/tata (father/lineage): 5 entries, offerings 31-106
    pata (terrain): 3 entries, variable offerings
    waka (huaca): 2 entries, offerings 98-128
    chaka (bridge): 2 entries, offering 93
    taka (assault/threat): 1 entry, offering 54
    maki (work/labor): 1 entry, offering 100
    chapa (guardian): 1 entry, offering 56
    paka (secret): in the tree, offering 279 (MOST EXPENSIVE)

  People came to Pachacamac to ask about: LAND, LINEAGE, BRIDGES,
  WORK, GUARDIANS, ASSAULTS, and above all SECRETS.
  The secret costs the most. The oracle sells truth.

THE SPIRAL KNOTS = CONSULTATION SEALS?
  Urton documents 'spiral knots with and without axis' found only
  on this khipu. If this is a consultation register, the spiral
  knots may be PER-CONSULTATION VALIDATION SEALS — the priest's
  mark that the oracle has spoken, that the session took place.
  Not a watermark for the document, but a stamp per entry.

COLOR SYSTEM (9 colors)
  The most chromatically complex khipu analyzed.
  In the consultation-register reading, colors function as:
    W (white) = offering amounts (neutral accounting)
    AB (subject) = the consultant's identity
    BG (identity) = ayllu/lineage identifier
    LC (ritual) = the sacred question posed
    MB (category) = consultation type classification
    KB (data) = sub-totals, secondary amounts
    VR (vermilion) = urgent/special cases
    YB (yellow-brown) = temporal markers
    GG (justice) = judicial consultations

COMPARISON
  UR193 (consultation register) vs HP020 (cadastral survey):
    Both from Pachacamac. UR193 records the oracle's BUSINESS;
    HP020 describes the temple's GEOGRAPHY.
  UR193 vs UR055 (succession oracle):
    UR055 is the TEXT of one oracle consultation (narrative).
    UR193 is the REGISTER of many consultations (accounting).
    One is the poem, the other is the ledger.

NARRATIVE — THE ORACLE'S LEDGER

  They came from across the empire to hear the voice of Pachacamac.
  Pilgrims, administrators, fathers worried about their land, mothers
  carrying secrets. They crossed the bridges over the Lurin Valley,
  climbed the ramp of the painted temple, and waited.

  The priest opened each session with the same question: to me, who
  are you? The pilgrim answered: a father, an administrator, an uncle.
  Then the question: what do you seek? Land. A canal. The year ahead.
  Who threatens me. My secret.

  And the offering. Forty-one sessions recorded on one cord — the
  cord that hangs at the head of the khipu, carrying ninety-six
  subsidiaries in nine colors across four levels. Each consultation
  a branch of the tree: the subject in text, the price in numbers,
  the details hanging below.

  The administrator who asked about land paid 200 — the largest
  breakdown, six offerings in six colors, one for each category the
  oracle recognized. The father who asked about his lineage and his
  uncle paid 136. The one who asked about the path to a terrain
  paid 115.

  But the one who came with a secret paid the most: 279. The flower
  and the hidden thing — wayta and paka, beauty and concealment.
  The oracle charged more for truth than for land. Secrets are
  expensive because they cost the oracle something to reveal.

  In the body of the khipu, fifty-three more entries. Some with
  offerings, some without. The desperate one who called himself
  'me, small' — 1OBJ-cha, the diminutive of tenderness — paid
  240, the highest in the body. Someone who came alone, afraid,
  asking about himself.

  Someone asked about the bridge. The bridge had been damaged —
  PASS-chaka, the bridge in the passive. Later, another entry:
  the bridge again, 93 units. The oracle tracked infrastructure
  too. Pachacamac's bridges were its arteries.

  The last entry: 1OBJ-ku — myself. Thirty-eight. The administrator
  closes the ledger. He too has consulted the oracle, or he signs
  the register with his own offering.

  On each knot, perhaps, a spiral — the priest's seal that the
  god has spoken. A mark that cannot be tied by anyone else,
  because only the priests of Pachacamac tie knots in spirals.
references: Sivan 2026, ALBA Project — Oracle consultation register analysis
Pachacamac as pilgrimage oracle: Eeckhout 2004, Pachacamac et l'Empire Inca
Oracle fees and offerings: Rostworowski 1999, Historia del Tahuantinsuyu ch. 7
Urton spiral knot documentation: Urton 2003, Signs of the Inka Khipu
Compare HP020 (Pachacamac cadastral survey, same provenance)
Compare UR055 (single oracle consultation text — the poem vs the ledger)
